text:
There once was a little mouse that had an extraordinary talent. Whenever he dreamed at midnight, a magnificent garden would materialize right there in his cellar. Every morning, the view would be just as lush and vibrant.

Once, our little friend dreamed so vividly it felt real; he decided to try something out. He filled his basket with tiny trinkets and left it outside his window. By morning light, a glorious garden awaited his return.

He discovered an old bottlecap flute; hummingbirds would dance just for him. A little seedpod orchestra practiced every dawn. And there was a pond with lily pads floating just like balloons.

He discovered butterflies that liked sweet honey on tiny sticks.

One evening, before bedtime stars twinkled lazily overhead.

In his sleepwalking state, he snuck out.

A gentle rain showered the garden.

The flowers danced.

When he awoke, the garden was exactly as he remembered the next day.

He did this many times.

One time he
